Usage: postman [options] [command]

Options:
  -v, --version                          output the version number
  --silent                               Silences all terminal output
  --color <value>                        Enable/Disable colored output (auto|on|off) (default: "auto")
  -h, --help                             display help for command

Commands:
  login [options]                        Authenticate with Postman to access your resources.
  logout [options]                       Delete the stored Postman API key.
  collection                             Run and test your Postman collections directly from the command line.
  api                                    Publish and test your APIs directly from the command line
  runner                                 Run runners on your own environments for monitoring your APIs
  spec                                   Lint and validate Specifications from the command line
  monitor                                Invoke a monitor run and display results
  workspace                              Manage workspace resources.
  performance                            Manage performance tests on your collections.
  flows|fl                               Manage and interact with flows.
  request [options] [methodOrUrl] [url]  Send HTTP requests from the command line with full Postman capabilities including authentication, environment variables, assertions, retries, and detailed output control.
  sdk                                    Generate and manage your SDKs directly from the command line.
  mock                                   Run and manage local Postman mock servers.
  application|app                        Application-level commands: initialise config and run tests.
  simulate                               Start mock servers with simulated failure scenarios.

To get available options for a command:
  postman <command> -h
